Popcorn Acreage, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 108–7, div. A, title VII, § 767117 Stat. 48Pub. L. 113–79, title I, § 1609(c)128 Stat. 709Feb. 20, 2003Feb. 7, 2014“Notwithstanding any other provision of law, for purposes of administering subtitle A of title I of the Agricultural Act of 2014 [ et seq.], acreage planted to, or prevented from being planted to, popcorn shall be considered as acreage planted to, or prevented from being planted to, corn: , That if a farm program payment yield for corn is otherwise established for a farm under such subtitle, the same yield shall be used for the acreage on the farm planted to, or prevented from being planted to, popcorn: , That with respect to all other farms, the farm program payment yield for such popcorn acreage shall be established by the Secretary on a fair and equitable basis to reflect the farm program payment yields for corn on similar farms in the area.”7 U.S.C. 9011ProvidedProvided further

children.heading: Actual crop revenue
children.label: 1 Actual crop revenue
children.text: The term “actual crop revenue”, with respect to a covered commodity for a crop year, means the amount determined by the Secretary under .section 9017(b) of this title

children.heading: Agriculture risk coverage
children.label: 2 Agriculture risk coverage
children.text: The term “agriculture risk coverage” means coverage provided under .section 9017 of this title

children.heading: Agriculture risk coverage guarantee
children.label: 3 Agriculture risk coverage guarantee
children.text: The term “agriculture risk coverage guarantee”, with respect to a covered commodity for a crop year, means the amount determined by the Secretary under .section 9017(c) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “base acres”, with respect to a covered commodity on a farm, means the number of acres in effect under sections 8702 and 8751 of this title, as adjusted pursuant to sections 8711, 8718, and 8752 of this title, as in effect on , subject to any reallocation, adjustment, or reduction under .September 30, 2013section 9012 of this title

children.heading: Inclusion of generic base acres
children.label: B Inclusion of generic base acres
children.text: The term “base acres” includes any generic base acres planted to a covered commodity as determined in .section 9014(b) of this title

children.heading: County coverage
children.label: 5 County coverage
children.text: The term “county coverage” means agriculture risk coverage selected under to be obtained at the county level.section 9015(b)(1) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “covered commodity” means wheat, oats, and barley (including wheat, oats, and barley used for haying and grazing), corn, grain sorghum, long grain rice, medium grain rice, pulse crops, soybeans, other oilseeds, and peanuts.

children.heading: Inclusion
children.label: B Inclusion
children.text: Effective beginning with the 2018 crop year, the term “covered commodity” includes seed cotton.

children.heading: Effective price
children.label: 7 Effective price
children.text: The term “effective price”, with respect to a covered commodity for a crop year, means the price calculated by the Secretary under to determine whether price loss coverage payments are required to be provided for that crop year.section 9016(b) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: An amount equal to 115 percent of the reference price for such covered commodity.

children.label: i
children.text: the reference price for such covered commodity; or

children.label: ii
children.text: beginning with the crop year 2025, 88 percent of the average of the marketing year average price of the covered commodity for the most recent 5 crop years, excluding each of the crop years with the highest and lowest marketing year average price.

children.label: A
children.text: is produced from pure strain varieties of the Barbadense species or any hybrid of the species, or other similar types of extra long staple cotton, designated by the Secretary, having characteristics needed for various end uses for which United States upland cotton is not suitable and grown in irrigated cotton-growing regions of the United States designated by the Secretary or other areas designated by the Secretary as suitable for the production of the varieties or types; and

children.label: B
children.text: is ginned on a roller-type gin or, if authorized by the Secretary, ginned on another type gin for experimental purposes.

children.heading: Generic base acres
children.label: 10 Generic base acres
children.text: The term “generic base acres” means the number of base acres for cotton in effect under , as adjusted pursuant to , as in effect on , subject to any adjustment or reduction under .section 8702 of this titlesection 8711 of this titlesection 9012 of this titleSeptember 30, 2013

children.heading: Individual coverage
children.label: 11 Individual coverage
children.text: The term “individual coverage” means agriculture risk coverage selected under to be obtained at the farm level.section 9015(b)(2) of this title

children.heading: Medium grain rice
children.label: 12 Medium grain rice
children.text: The term “medium grain rice” includes short grain rice and temperate japonica rice.

children.heading: Other oilseed
children.label: 13 Other oilseed
children.text: The term “other oilseed” means a crop of sunflower seed, rapeseed, canola, safflower, flaxseed, mustard seed, crambe, sesame seed, or any oilseed designated by the Secretary.

children.heading: Payment acres
children.label: 14 Payment acres
children.text: The term “payment acres”, with respect to the provision of price loss coverage payments and agriculture risk coverage payments, means the number of acres determined for a farm under .section 9014 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: means the yield used to make payments pursuant to section 8714 or 8754 of this title, as in effect on ; orSeptember 30, 2013

children.label: B
children.text: means the yield established under .section 9013 of this title

children.heading: Price loss coverage
children.label: 16 Price loss coverage
children.text: The term “price loss coverage” means coverage provided under .section 9016 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “producer” means an owner, operator, landlord, tenant, or sharecropper that shares in the risk of producing a crop and is entitled to share in the crop available for marketing from the farm, or would have shared had the crop been produced.

children.label: i
children.text: not take into consideration the existence of a hybrid seed contract; and

children.label: ii
children.text: ensure that program requirements do not adversely affect the ability of the grower to receive a payment under this chapter.

children.heading: Pulse crop
children.label: 18 Pulse crop
children.text: The term “pulse crop” means dry peas, lentils, small chickpeas, and large chickpeas.

children.label: i
children.text: For wheat, $6.35 per bushel.

children.label: ii
children.text: For corn, $4.10 per bushel.

children.label: iii
children.text: For grain sorghum, $4.40 per bushel.

children.label: iv
children.text: For barley, $5.45 per bushel.

children.label: v
children.text: For oats, $2.65 per bushel.

children.label: vi
children.text: For long grain rice, $16.90 per hundredweight.

children.label: vii
children.text: For medium grain rice, $16.90 per hundredweight.

children.label: viii
children.text: For soybeans, $10.00 per bushel.

children.label: ix
children.text: For other oilseeds, $23.75 per hundredweight.

children.label: x
children.text: For peanuts, $630.00 per ton.

children.label: xi
children.text: For dry peas, $13.10 per hundredweight.

children.label: xii
children.text: For lentils, $23.75 per hundredweight.

children.label: xiii
children.text: For small chickpeas, $22.65 per hundredweight.

children.label: xiv
children.text: For large chickpeas, $25.65 per hundredweight.

children.label: xv
children.text: For seed cotton, $0.42 per pound.

children.heading: Effectiveness
children.label: B Effectiveness
children.text: Effective beginning with the 2031 crop year, the reference prices defined in subparagraph (A) with respect to a covered commodity shall equal the reference price in the previous crop year multiplied by 1.005.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: C Limitation
children.text: In no case shall a reference price for a covered commodity exceed 113 percent of the reference price for such covered commodity listed in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 20 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Agriculture.

children.heading: Seed cotton
children.label: 21 Seed cotton
children.text: The term “seed cotton” means unginned upland cotton that includes both lint and seed.

children.label: A
children.text: a State;

children.label: B
children.text: the District of Columbia;

children.label: C
children.text: the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico; and

children.label: D
children.text: any other territory or possession of the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: the reallocation of base acres under ;section 9012 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the establishment of a reference price (as required under ) and an effective price pursuant to ; andsection 9016(g) of this titlesection 9016 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: the determination of the actual crop revenue and agriculture risk coverage guarantee pursuant to .section 9017 of this title

children.heading: Transitional yield
children.label: 24 Transitional yield
children.text: The term “transitional yield” has the meaning given the term in .section 1502(b) of this title

children.heading: United States
children.label: 25 United States
children.text: The term “United States”, when used in a geographical sense, means all of the States.

children.heading: United States Premium Factor
children.label: 26 United States Premium Factor
children.text: The term “United States Premium Factor” means the percentage by which the difference in the United States loan schedule premiums for Strict Middling (SM) 1⅛-inch upland cotton and for Middling (M) 1⁄-inch upland cotton exceeds the difference in the applicable premiums for comparable international qualities.332
